figures.caption: Table 1. Illustrating and comparing predictive performance and covariate selection behavior of penalized (logistic) regression methods across four simulation examples (𝑝 = 1,000, 𝑛 = 200). Reported values are median (standard deviation) over 1000 replications. AUC: area under the ROC curve; ME: misclassification error; |𝑆 |: number of selected predictors.

Table 5. Comparing naive and corrected sparse estimators in a high-dimensional setting with measurement error (𝑠 = 10, 𝑝 = 5,000, 𝑛 = 200, Σ𝑈 known). Reported values are mean (standard deviation) over 1,000 replications. |𝑆 |: total variables selected; #C: number of true positives. True lasso serves as an oracle benchmark fitted on the error-free covariates 𝑋 .

Table 4. Comparing naive and corrected sparse estimators in a high-dimensional setting with measurement error (𝑠 = 5, 𝑝 = 5,000, 𝑛 = 200, Σ𝑈 known). Reported values are mean (standard deviation) over 1,000 replications. |𝑆 |: total variables selected; #C: number of true positives. True lasso serves as an oracle benchmark fitted on the error-free covariates 𝑋 .
